pattern: Precomputing per-row and per-column one-counts lets each cell's difference be assembled in constant time, since zeros are just the dimension minus the ones.
hints: Every cell's value depends only on totals for its row and its column, so compute those totals once before filling the answer.
The zeros in a row equal the row width minus its ones, and likewise for a column, so you only need to count ones.
After tallying ones per row and per column, set diff[i][j] = onesRow[i] + onesCol[j] - zerosRow[i] - zerosCol[j], expanding zeros via the dimensions.

stress.description: random binary grid with bounded area
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 40)))
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 40)))
    grid = [[rng.randint(0, 1) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
stress.note: area kept modest so the reference stays fast
